title: Cirrus: Support testing of VM cache-image changes
body:
Previously, it was quite difficult to affect changes to VM cache images
without lots of manual work.  This commit adds a new optional testing
task which mirrors the official-image build task which only runs on
master.  In contrast, the new task may be run at any time in a PR, but
including a magic phrase in the PR description:

Update documentation to describe the new task and inform on it's usage.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: /hold
/cc @baude

Okay, so the[ task output from Cirrus ](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5184911836184576)was:

```

--> ubuntu-18: 
--> rhel-7: Exported artifacts in: [gs://packer-import/rhel-7-libpod-9d05a268.tar.gz]
--> rhel-7: 
--> fedora-29: Exported artifacts in: [gs://packer-import/fedora-29-libpod-9d05a268.tar.gz]
--> fedora-29: 
--> fedora-28: Exported artifacts in: [gs://packer-import/fedora-28-libpod-9d05a268.tar.gz]
--> fedora-28: 
```

Those can get convered into images for use in `.cirrus.yml` with the `gcloud` command I documented.  Otherwise they expire automatically from the `packer-import` bucket.

Those names can be dropped in to `.cirrus.yml` in this, or in a new PR, and stand a good chance of passing all tests.  I'm open to suggestions on simplifying/streamlining this workflow, but would prefer to do that in a follow-up PR (since this one was REALLY hard to get into a functional state).

@edsantiago thanks so much for the helpful feedback.  I've moved them all into a little todo list (above) that captures them, for  while I apply the changes.

@edsantiago all green :clap: I even updated `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` to support passing `SPECIALMODE` so VMs can be spun-up to mimic CI through the `setup_environment.sh`, ready for debugging and issue reproduction.

@baude @mheon PTAL, this is a big-one, though it does come with docs, and many months of rebasing, care and feeding.  With this PR, everybody can manage the VM-Cache images and validate the changes from the safety of a PR.  Nothing goes "live" until produced image names are manually added into ``.cirrus.yml``.

@cevich it looks like you missed my question yesterday: can you point me to logs of the errors you saw with `req_env_var`? I'm asking because I believe it's pretty important to implement it the way I suggested. Have you actually tested the function? In particular, have you tested that it actually works as expected? I believe it will not, because you are doing `while xxx; do ... if yyy; then exit`, and that's a classic pitfall. Could you try my suggestion again? Perhaps the failures you saw indicate a real problem.

Question - does this work for creating new cached images, not just existing ones? If I wanted to, say, tweak the F28 job into an F30 job, would I be able to accomplish it with these scripts, or is that a more manual thing?

> it looks like you missed my question yesterday: can you point me to logs of the errors you saw with req_env_var?

@edsantiago yes I did miss that.  I produced the errors when running `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` to test `lib.sh` changes.  I'm positive it was something related to other problems (now fixed) and my lack of bash-knowledge.  What we have in here now appears to work, but is not ideal.  It's probably something best left to a dedicated PR, where improvements can be validated independently.

@cevich sorry, it looks like I wasn't clear enough. What I'm trying to say is that the `req_env_var` code, as it is, does not work. It is broken in two ways, and you can confirm that easily, just save this as foo.sh and execute it:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

source contrib/cirrus/lib.sh
req_env_var "fixme" "THIS_IS_PROBABLY_NOT_SET $THIS_IS_PROBABLY_NOT_SET"

echo uh-oh looks like we did not exit after all
exit 0
```
The first broken, obvious, is that the code is echoing `$2` instead of `$1`. IOW: the entire `req_env_var` is a NOP. It is not testing anything.

The second broken is more subtle. You are doing an `exit` inside a subshell run as a pipe:
```bash
echo hi | while read line; do
    exit 2
done
```
This is one of the classic gotchas of bash programming. It has bitten me many times and will probably get me again some day. It is not fixable.

You can fix the entire problem, and get rid of evil duplication as a bonus, by doing:
```bash
req_env_var() {
    ...
    for i; do
        if [ -z "${!i}" ]; then
            echo "FATAL: $caller requires \$$i to be non-empty" >&2
            exit 9
        fi
    done
}
```
(Took the liberty of adding `>&2` which perhaps might not be appropriate for CI environment; adjust as necessary)
(Also took the liberty of removing unnecessary 'env. var.' because there isn't a UNIX user alive who wouldn't find it obvious from the dollar sign).
I hope this is clearer. If not, please ping me again because this is important.

(Given the scope of the `req_env_var` change I think it might make sense to submit that as a new fast-track PR, then, once that's merged, rebase this one. It would greatly simplify life for reviewers).

The '****' addition is nice but it breaks unit tests. 

Changes LGTM on first pass, but diffs are more complex than I expected so I'll need to make another pass; hopefully tonight.

It ain't pretty, but the simplest fix I can think of for unit tests is to fix check_result():
```
+    local actual=$(echo "$1" | tr -d '*>\012'|sed -e 's/^ \+//')
+    local expect=$2
```
...and replace $1/$2 with $actual/$expect in the next few lines.

@edsantiago and of course we get all the way through and fail in success.sh anyway :confused:   Simple typo, but yeah, there are a few places where being able to see that 'die' output clearly, is important (since more output follows it).  We're also not running the unit-tests in this image-test "mode" by design.  So I should probably remove the magic string to let the normal gating/lint-checking also take a stab.

This almost passes (still get two rc test failures).

```diff
diff --git a/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh.t b/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh.t
index ce51f8ad..28f28839 100755
--- a/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh.t
+++ b/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh.t
@@ -4,6 +4,8 @@
 #
 source $(dirname $0)/lib.sh
 
+set -o pipefail
+
 # Iterator and return code; updated in test functions
 testnum=0
 rc=0
@@ -20,6 +22,11 @@ function check_result {
     fi
 }
 
+function decoration_filter {
+    cat - | grep -v '\*' | sed -r -e 's/^>+ //'
+    return $?
+}
+
 ###############################################################################
 # tests for die()
 
@@ -30,7 +37,7 @@ function test_die() {
     local expected_msg=$4
 
     local msg
-    msg=$(die $input_status "$input_msg")
+    msg=$(die $input_status "$input_msg" | decoration_filter )
     local status=$?
 
     check_result "$msg" "$expected_msg" "die $input_status $input_msg"
@@ -50,7 +57,7 @@ function test_rev() {
 
     # bash gotcha: doing 'local msg=...' on one line loses exit status
     local msg
-    msg=$(req_env_var $input_args)
+    msg=$(req_env_var $input_args | decoration_filter)
     local status=$?
 
     check_result "$msg"    "$expected_msg"    "req_env_var $input_args"
```
(``--force` pushing that for now to confirm my `success.sh` changes are fine)
